engineIntelligence.h2: MG HS Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the MG HS in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: MG HS 15E4E engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - tensioner wear causes rattle and potential failure from 60k miles.
Turbo wastegate rattle - actuator wear leads to boost loss and reduced performance.
High-pressure fuel pump failure - common on higher-mileage direct-injection units.

cards.altText: MG HS 20L4E engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear - oil starvation causes premature failure on short-journey cars.
Timing chain tensioner fatigue - similar to 1.5L but less frequently reported.

cards.altText: MG HS 15S4E engine - hybrid 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Inverter coolant seal degradation - leaks into generator cause hybrid system shutdowns.
Crankshaft position sensor faults - intermittent stalling and no-start conditions.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £3,200-£5,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 15S4E units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: MG HS Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the MG HS across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The primary 1.5L turbo unit powering most UK MG HS models, offering strong mid-range torque but known for timing chain sensitivity.
compatibleVariants: SE, Excite, Exclusive, Trophy, Essence, Explore
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented from 60k miles, causes rattle on cold start
Turbo wastegate actuator failure - leads to boost loss and reduced power
High-pressure fuel pump degradation - affects direct-injection performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for 15E4E MG HS engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: Variant of the 1.5L turbo family with revised calibration, fitted to early UK HS models before 15E4E standardisation.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch - similar tensioner weakness to 15E4E
Carbon buildup on intake valves - common on direct-injection units with short journeys
entries.cta: Get quotes for 15S4G MG HS engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: Less common 1.5L turbo code, primarily fitted to specific export-market HS units later sold in UK.
commonFailures: Oil consumption increase - piston ring land design on higher-mileage examples
EGR valve clogging - affects emissions and idle quality
entries.cta: Get quotes for 15C4E MG HS engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The higher-output 2.0L turbo option for performance-focused HS buyers, delivering stronger acceleration but with higher replacement costs.
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear - oil starvation risk on short-journey driving
Timing chain tensioner fatigue - less frequent than 1.5L but still documented
entries.cta: Get quotes for 20L4E MG HS engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Alternative 2.0L turbo code used in limited HS production runs, sharing core architecture with 20L4E.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - similar to 1.5L direct-injection units
Intercooler hose detachment - rare but reported on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for 16K4F MG HS engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Hybrid
entries.history: The plug-in hybrid powertrain combining a 1.5L turbo petrol engine with electric motor, offering reduced emissions but complex replacement requirements.
commonFailures: Inverter coolant seal degradation - leaks cause hybrid system shutdowns per TSB guidance
Crankshaft position sensor faults - intermittent no-start or stalling conditions
entries.cta: Get quotes for 15S4E MG HS engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Hybrid
entries.history: Revised hybrid code for later PHEV HS models with updated battery management integration.
commonFailures: Generator coupling wear - causes vibration and reduced electric assist
CAN bus communication faults - affects hybrid system coordination
entries.cta: Get quotes for 15FDE MG HS engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: MG HS Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The MG HS 1.5L turbo petrol engines (15E4E, 15S4G) dominate UK replacement demand, with timing chain stretch the most reported failure. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to compare repair vs replacement costs and make an informed decision.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why MG HS 1.5L Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: MG HS SE, Excite, Exclusive, Trophy, Essence (2019-2024, 15E4E & 15S4G)
problems.rootCause: Inadequate oil flow to tensioner on cold starts causes premature wear, leading to chain stretch and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain kit with updated tensioner, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before valve contact; adds 40-60k miles if oil changes maintained

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt unit with reinforced timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution for failed engines; eliminates recurrence risk with upgraded parts

problems.recommendation: If your MG HS shows early chain rattle below 70k miles, a preventative replacement offers better value than waiting for catastrophic failure. Once valves are bent, full engine replacement is the only viable option - and often more cost-effective than repairing a damaged block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 15E4E engine prices for your MG HS

problems.h4: Turbo Wastegate Failure - Loss of Power on MG HS 1.5L & 2.0L
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator diaphragm fatigue causes boost pressure loss, triggering limp mode and reduced acceleration.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Actuator Replacement - Replace wastegate actuator only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace actuator assembly, recalibrate boost map
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if turbo housing is sound; may recur if core turbo is worn

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Turbo or Engine - Fit refurbished turbo or complete rebuilt engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply refurbished turbo with new actuator OR complete rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo
repairOptions.longevity: Rebuilt engine eliminates turbo risk entirely; preferred for high-mileage HS units

problems.recommendation: For MG HS owners over 70k miles, investing in a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components often provides better long-term value than repeated actuator replacements.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MG HS turbo replacement or rebuilt engine

problems.h4: High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Direct Injection Issues
problems.rootCause: HPFP internal wear reduces fuel pressure, causing misfires, rough idle, and potential catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - HPFP Replacement - Replace high-pressure fuel pump only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, replace HPFP, recalibrate fuel trims
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before secondary damage; pump quality varies by supplier

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded fuel system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with OEM-spec HPFP and fuel rail, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for high-mileage HS where multiple components show wear

problems.recommendation: If your MG HS shows fuel trim errors alongside HPFP codes, a rebuilt engine addresses the root cause and prevents repeat failures.
problems.cta: Compare MG HS 1.5L rebuilt engine prices with upgraded fuel system

problems.h4: Hybrid Inverter Coolant Leak - PHEV System Shutdown
problems.rootCause: Inverter coolant seal degradation allows fluid into generator, triggering hybrid system fault codes and no-start conditions.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Seal & Inverter Service - Replace coolant seals, flush system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove inverter, replace seals, pressure test hybrid system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if generator is undamaged; requires IMI-certified technician

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Hybrid Powertrain - Supply & fit rebuilt hybrid unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt hybrid powertrain with updated seals, full HV diagnostics, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution for PHEV HS with confirmed inverter damage

problems.recommendation: For PHEV MG HS with confirmed coolant ingress, a rebuilt hybrid powertrain offers the only reliable long-term fix - standard repair rarely prevents recurrence.
problems.cta: Enquire about MG HS PHEV hybrid engine replacement options

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your MG HS
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain, turbo, fuel pump or hybrid inverter issues don't have to mean scrapping your MG HS. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Compare prices, confirm warranty terms, and choose supply-only or nationwide supply & fit - all with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare MG HS Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific MG HS and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.description: The 1.5-litre turbo petrol engine (15E4E) is the backbone of the MG HS range in the UK, powering most SE, Trophy and Exclusive variants from 2019 onwards with strong mid-range torque for urban and motorway driving.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear from 60k miles

commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger bearing wear on short-journey use
timing chain tensioner fatigue less frequent than 1.5L

engineYears.h2: MG HS Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the MG HS production timeline (2019-present), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The MG HS launched in the UK with 1.5L and 2.0L turbo petrol engines, establishing the 15E4E as the dominant replacement code.
keyChanges: UK market launch with 1.5L T-GDI (15E4E) as standard engine
Optional 2.0L T-GDI (20L4E) on high-spec Trophy and Exclusive trims
Introduction of Excite, Exclusive and Trophy trim levels
knownFor: Strong initial sales driving early replacement demand
First reports of timing chain rattle on 1.5L units approaching 60k miles
Turbo wastegate issues emerging on high-mileage 2.0L examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2021 MG HS replacement engines
years.ticker: MG HS 1.5 engine replacement • MG HS timing chain noise • MG HS Trophy engine cost

years.description: Production stabilised with the 15E4E confirmed as the primary engine code, while PHEV variants introduced hybrid complexity.
keyChanges: PHEV (plug-in hybrid) variants launched with 15S4E engine code
Trim simplification: Excite/Exclusive phased out in favour of SE/Trophy
Updated ECU calibration for improved emissions compliance
knownFor: Timing chain failure reports increase on 2019-2020 1.5L units
PHEV inverter coolant leak issues documented in owner forums
Strong used market demand driving replacement engine enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-2023 MG HS replacement engines
years.ticker: MG HS PHEV engine replacement • MG HS 15E4E timing chain • MG HS SE engine cost

years.description: The facelifted MG HS retains the 1.5L turbo architecture with revised calibration, while hybrid options gain prominence in the UK lineup.
keyChanges: Updated front-end design and interior technology
Revised 15E4E calibration for improved fuel economy
PHEV variants continue with updated battery management (15FDE code)
knownFor: Early reliability data still emerging for facelift models
Replacement demand remains focused on pre-2024 15E4E units
Hybrid system complexity continues to drive specialist enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2024 MG HS replacement engines

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your MG HS was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK MG HS specialists.

faq.h2: MG HS Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace an MG HS engine in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt MG HS engine prices range from £1,850-£2,400 for 1.5L petrol units and £2,400-£3,500 for 2.0L or hybrid variants, supply only.
keyPoints: 1.5L 15E4E rebuilt engines: £1,850-£2,400 typical range
2.0L 20L4E units cost more due to lower availability
Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 depending on garage location
All prices include 12-month warranty from vetted UK suppliers
items.cta: Compare live MG HS engine replacement quotes now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an MG HS?
items.answer: Yes, if your MG HS is under 8 years old or has strong service history - a rebuilt engine costs far less than buying a replacement vehicle.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine: £1,850-£3,500 vs £15k+ for equivalent used HS
Timing chain failure doesn't always mean total write-off
Rebuilt units include upgraded components addressing original failure
12-month warranty provides peace of mind vs uncertain used purchase
items.cta: Find out if engine replacement makes sense for your MG HS

items.question: What causes timing chain failure on MG HS 1.5L engines?
items.answer: Inadequate oil flow to the tensioner on cold starts causes premature wear, leading to chain stretch and potential valve damage if ignored.
keyPoints: Most common on 15E4E and 15S4G codes from 60k miles
Rattle on cold start is the key early warning symptom
Regular oil changes with correct spec can delay onset
Preventative replacement costs less than repairing valve damage
items.cta: Get quotes for MG HS timing chain or rebuilt engine replacement

items.question: Can I fit a used MG HS engine instead of rebuilt?
items.answer: Yes, used engines cost less (£1,200-£1,900) but carry unknown history and shorter warranties versus rebuilt units with upgraded components.
keyPoints: Used engines: cheaper upfront but verify mileage and service history
Rebuilt engines: include new timing components and 12-month warranty
For high-mileage HS, rebuilt often offers better long-term value
Always request compression test report for used units
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt MG HS engine options and pricing

items.question: How long does an MG HS engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With regular oil changes and timely timing chain attention, MG HS 1.5L engines can reach 120k-150k miles before major work is needed.
keyPoints: Follow MG's 10k-mile oil change interval strictly
Use correct 0W-20 or 5W-30 fully synthetic oil spec
Listen for cold-start rattle indicating chain tensioner wear
Address turbo or fuel pump issues early to avoid secondary damage
items.cta: Check MG HS engine longevity tips and maintenance guides

items.question: Do MG HS replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all rebuilt and reconditioned MG HS engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Warranty covers major components excluding wear items like belts
Hybrid units include HV system functionality guarantee
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: View MG HS engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt MG HS engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines replace worn parts to working spec; rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points like timing chains.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: cost-effective, suitable for lower-mileage replacements
Rebuilt: includes reinforced timing kits, better for high-mileage HS
Both undergo bench testing and carry 12-month warranty
Price difference typically £200-£400 favouring rebuilt for longevity
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt MG HS engine prices today

items.question: How quickly can I get a replacement MG HS engine?
items.answer: Most rebuilt MG HS engines are dispatched within 2-5 working days UK-wide, with supply & fit appointments available within 1-2 weeks.
keyPoints: Standard rebuilt units: 2-5 day dispatch from UK stock
Hybrid/PHEV engines may require longer lead time
Supply & fit scheduling depends on local garage availability
Enter registration for exact lead time on your variant
items.cta: Check live stock and delivery times for your MG HS engine

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my MG HS insurance or MOT?
items.answer: No, a like-for-like replacement engine with matching code won't affect MOT or insurance, but you must declare the change to your insurer.
keyPoints: MOT testers check emissions, not engine serial numbers
Insurers require notification of major component changes
Keep invoice and warranty documentation for future reference
Ensure ECU coding matches replacement engine specification
items.cta: Get advice on MG HS engine replacement documentation

items.question: How do I find my MG HS engine code before ordering?
items.answer: Check the stamped metal plate on the engine block near the exhaust manifold, or use your registration number for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: Common codes: 15E4E (1.5L), 20L4E (2.0L), 15S4E (PHEV)
Registration lookup via our tool confirms exact engine fitment
VIN decoding also reveals factory-fitted engine specification
Always verify code before ordering to avoid compatibility issues
items.cta: Identify your MG HS engine code instantly with our free tool
